sanskrit:
संजय उवाच
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्।
विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः॥
transliteration:
sañjaya uvāca
taṃ tathā kṛpayāviṣṭam aśru-pūrṇākulekṣaṇam
viṣīdantam idaṃ vākyam uvāca madhusūdanaḥ
literal_meaning: Sanjaya said: To him—thus overcome by compassion, eyes turbid with tears, despondent—Madhusūdana spoke these words.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s visible emotional collapse (tears, silence, trembling) signals a full-blown acute stress reaction. Krishna’s entry marks the shift from battlefield narrative to inner therapy session.
psychological_state: Acute moral injury with dissociative shutdown; compassion misdirected toward transient bodies rather than eternal selves.
trigger_scenario: A paramedic freezes at a mass-casualty scene, unable to triage because every victim reminds her of her own child.
anchor_text: When despair floods, the first step is to speak your pain aloud.
